summary:
[Only two concise sentences here.]

Summary: The predominant connective tissue cells in the periodontal ligament are fibroblasts, which produce collagen fibers crucial for anchoring teeth to bones. These cells arise from the dental follicle and maintain periodontal integrity through response to occlusal stresses.
